## Summary - Problem: xCloud hosting documentation was missing from the OpenClaw docs - Why it matters: Users deploying on xCloud need official documentation following the same format as other VPS providers (Railway, Northflank, etc.) - What changed: Added comprehensive xCloud hosting guide at docs/install/xcloud.mdx, updated VPS hub page to include xCloud, and added navigation entries in docs.json - What did NOT change: No code changes, only documentation additions ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Docs ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] UI / DX ## Linked Issue/PR - Related: N/A (community contribution) ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - New documentation page available at /install/xcloud - xCloud added to VPS hosting options on /vps page - Navigation updated to include xCloud in the hosting section - Redirects added for /xcloud and /platforms/xcloud ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No - New/changed network calls? No - Command/tool execution surface changed? No - Data access scope changed? No ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - Documentation only change - Verified Mintlify format matches existing docs (northflank.mdx, railway.mdx) ### Steps 1. Navigate to docs.openclaw.ai/vps 2. Click on xCloud link 3. Review xCloud hosting guide at docs.openclaw.ai/install/xcloud ### Expected - xCloud appears in VPS provider list - xCloud documentation page renders correctly with proper formatting - All internal and external links work properly - Navigation structure is consistent with other VPS providers ### Actual - Documentation follows established patterns - Formatting matches other VPS provider docs - Navigation structure is consistent - External links point to official xCloud resources ## Evidence - [x] Documentation follows existing format (northflank.mdx, railway.mdx) - [x] Added to navigation in docs.json - [x] Updated VPS hub page with xCloud entry - [x] Includes redirects for /xcloud and /platforms/xcloud - [x] External links added to official xCloud resources ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Documentation structure matches existing VPS guides (Northflank, Railway) - All frontmatter is properly formatted (MDX format) - Content is comprehensive and follows OpenClaw documentation style - Navigation entries added correctly in docs.json - External links point to correct xCloud resources - Edge cases checked: - Redirect paths added for common URL patterns (/xcloud, /platforms/xcloud) - Proper ordering in VPS provider list (after Northflank, before Oracle) - MDX file extension used (consistent with other one-click deploy guides) - What you did **not** verify: - Live deployment on Mintlify (will be verified after merge) - Actual xCloud deployment flow (documentation based on provided guide) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? Yes - Config/env changes? No - Migration needed? No ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Remove docs/install/xcloud.mdx and revert changes to docs/vps.md and docs/docs.json - Files/config to restore: - docs/vps.md (remove xCloud entry from provider list) - docs/docs.json (remove xCloud from navigation and redirects) - docs/install/xcloud.mdx (delete file) - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - Broken links in navigation - 404 errors on /install/xcloud - Formatting issues in Mintlify ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Documentation may not perfectly match actual xCloud implementation details - Mitigation: Based on official xCloud deployment guide provided by contributor; can be updated based on user feedback and xCloud team input - Risk: External links may change or break over time - Mitigation: Links point to official xCloud documentation and hosting pages; xCloud team can notify if URLs change ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed**: OpenClaw docs had no xCloud hosting guide. This PR adds `docs/install/xcloud.mdx`, exposes it via the VPS hub `` in `docs/vps.md`, registers `/xcloud` and `/platforms/xcloud` redirects in `docs/docs.json`, and slots `install/xcloud` into the Hosting navigation group alphabetically. - **Real environment tested**: Local macOS workstation against the rebased branch tip `b2fd437e64` (single commit on top of `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `3756e5dc10`). Verified directly against the committed files using `node` one-liners, the rebase replay with `git`, force-push via `gh`, and the public xCloud OpenClaw hosting URL via the web. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ``` # 1. JSON validity and xCloud routing node -e "const j=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/docs.json','utf-8')); console.log(j.redirects.filter(r=>r.source.includes('xcloud') || r.destination.includes('xcloud')));" # 2. MDX structural inspection (frontmatter, headings, external links) node -e "const b=require('fs').readFileSync('docs/install/xcloud.mdx','utf-8'); console.log({lines:b.split('\n').length, headings:[...b.matchAll(/^#{1,6}\s+(.*)$/gm)].length, ext:[...b.matchAll(/\]\((https?:\/\/[^)]+)\)/g)].map(m=>m[1])});" # 3. Hosting nav contains install/xcloud node -e "const j=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/docs.json','utf-8')); function f(n,name){if(Array.isArray(n)){for(const it of n){const r=f(it,name);if(r)return r;}return null;} if(typeof n==='object'){if(n.group===name)return n; for(const v of Object.values(n)){const r=f(v,name);if(r)return r;}} return null;} const h=f(j.navigation,'Hosting'); console.log('install/xcloud in Hosting nav:', h.pages.includes('install/xcloud'), 'at index', h.pages.indexOf('install/xcloud'));" # 4. Diff scope against upstream main git diff --stat upstream/main..HEAD -- docs/ # 5. GitHub Actions status on the rebased commit gh pr checks 17098 --repo openclaw/openclaw ``` - **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured from the steps above against the rebased branch tip: ``` $ node -e "...xCloud redirects from docs.json..." [ { source: '/xcloud', destination: '/install/xcloud' }, { source: '/platforms/xcloud', destination: '/install/xcloud' } ] $ node -e "...xcloud.mdx structural inspection..." { lines: 155, headings: 20, ext: [ 'https://xcloud.host/openclaw-hosting/', 'https://xcloud.host/docs/how-to-deploy-openclaw-with-xcloud/', 'https://xcloud.host/docs/how-to-deploy-openclaw-with-xcloud/' ] } $ node -e "...Hosting nav membership..." install/xcloud in Hosting nav: true at index 16 $ git diff --stat upstream/main..HEAD -- docs/ docs/docs.json | 11 +++++++++++ docs/install/xcloud.mdx | 154 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ docs/vps.md | 1 + 3 files changed, 165 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-) $ gh pr checks 17098 --repo openclaw/openclaw | awk -F'\t' '{print $2}' | sort | uniq -c 1 fail # this Real behavior proof gate 11 pass 15 skipping # path-filtered for docs-only diff ``` External xCloud-side reference for the partnership this guide documents: . Deployment walkthrough on xCloud's own docs: . - **Observed result after fix**: All three docs surfaces carry the expected xCloud routing on the rebased tip. `docs.json` parses cleanly and contains both xCloud redirects plus `install/xcloud` in the Hosting `pages` array at index 16 (alphabetically last after `install/render`). `docs/install/xcloud.mdx` parses as a 155-line MDX file with valid frontmatter, 20 headings, three external links all pointing at `xcloud.host`, and no broken internal links. `docs/vps.md` adds exactly one new `` slotted after Northflank in the existing ``. The previously-failing `check-test-types` job is no longer triggered for this docs-only diff (path-filter skip), and steipete's fix `e520d9b8f6` on `openclaw/openclaw:main` means the underlying type regression would not recur even if it ran. - **What was not tested**: An end-to-end OpenClaw deployment on a real xCloud-managed server, since I do not have an xCloud account or test credentials. Production Mintlify rendering at `docs.openclaw.ai/install/xcloud`, which only resolves after merge. The Mintlify `dev` server was not run locally because it needs the docs publish-repo toolchain rather than the OpenClaw monorepo dev setup.

Greptile Summary

Adds a new xCloud hosting guide (`docs/install/xcloud.mdx`), updates the VPS hub page (`docs/vps.md`) to include xCloud in the provider list, and adds navigation entries and redirects in `docs/docs.json`. The guide covers managed VPS deployment through xCloud's dashboard, including server provisioning, AI provider configuration, Telegram integration, and post-deployment management. - Documentation-only change; no code modifications - Navigation and redirect entries follow established patterns (consistent with Railway, Northflank, etc.) - Previous review threads flagged issues with "Claude Code" incorrectly listed as an AI provider, a missing word ("if") on line 43, and redundant "optional" phrasing on line 28 — these should be addressed before merging

Confidence Score: 4/5

- Documentation-only PR with no code changes; safe to merge after addressing previously flagged text issues. - This is a docs-only change adding a new hosting guide. The navigation and redirect changes in docs.json and vps.md are clean and follow established patterns. The xcloud.mdx file has a few text issues (already flagged in previous review threads) that should be resolved, but none are blocking or introduce risk. No code, security, or runtime concerns. - `docs/install/xcloud.mdx` needs the previously flagged text corrections (Claude Code provider error, missing word, redundant phrasing) addressed before merge. target.title: fix(cron): suppress fatal error completion announce
target.body:
Fixes #42243.

## Summary
- short-circuit normal cron completion delivery only when an isolated run resolves to a fatal structured `isError` payload
- keep controlled fatal failure signals on the existing delivery path, preserving existing cron behavior
- keep the cron run marked as error with diagnostics and delivery trace state, but avoid direct announce fallback with provider error JSON
- rebase the PR on current `origin/main` so the branch no longer depends on the old base

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: A cron isolated-agent run with `delivery.mode=announce` and a fatal structured `isError` payload should not send provider error JSON to the configured channel as a normal cron completion message. Controlled synthesized fatal failure signals should still use the existing delivery path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `2e6ca9f703`, using the repo cron test runner against the actual isolated-agent delivery policy path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  37 passed (37)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 17.90s

$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: The new fatal structured error payload path skips `dispatchCronDelivery`, while the existing synthesized fatal failure signal case still dispatches through the controlled delivery path.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps in the changed pre-transport cron delivery decision point; external channel credentials are outside this branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, fatal structured `isError` payloads could continue into normal announce completion delivery and expose provider error JSON.

## Testing
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts
- git diff --check

target.title: fix(codex): block progress-only completions [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ACP/Codex and subagent child tasks could be recorded as normal success when the captured completion was only progress text, missing, or never delivered back to the requester.
- Solution: add a narrow required-completion contract that maps missing/progress-only required output and exhausted required delivery to the existing blocked terminal outcome.
- What changed: ACP background task finalization and subagent lifecycle finalization now use the same completion contract; focused regressions cover progress-only, missing output, delivery failure, and real final report preservation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no channel transport rewrite, no provider behavior change, no task status enum change, no new dependency, no external service or credential handling change.

## Motivation

#83863 is a message-loss/session-state bug: a child task can appear successful even though the user never got a real final deliverable. That makes follow-up decisions unsafe because the durable task record says "ok" while the useful completion either never existed or never reached the requester.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83863
- Related #80879
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Required ACP/Codex and subagent child tasks could end as normal success when the captured completion was only progress text, missing, or when required completion delivery was exhausted.
- Real environment tested: Fresh OpenClaw checkout on Linux with `origin/main @ 0ab1449215` plus this patch head `cac6e1f842`, Node v22.22.2, temporary OpenClaw state directory, no persistent services.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a diagnostic runtime script through the real OpenClaw ACP Manager, Task Registry, and Subagent Lifecycle source paths: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp-state> node --import tsx ../proof.mjs`. Also ran the plugin-owned Codex harness subagent-only live check with `node scripts/test-live.mjs --codex-harness -- src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live output from the after-fix diagnostic run:

  ```text
  proof_timestamp=2026-05-21T21:50:08.704Z
  acp_progress_only: {"status":"succeeded","terminalOutcome":"blocked","progressSummary":"I'll inspect the repo now.","terminalSummary":"Required completion ended with progress-only text, not a final deliverable.","deliveryStatus":"session_queued"}
  acp_final_report: {"status":"succeeded","progressSummary":"Fixed the crash and verified the regression tests pass.","deliveryStatus":"session_queued"}
  subagent_progress_only: {"status":"succeeded","terminalOutcome":"blocked","progressSummary":"I'll inspect the repo now.","terminalSummary":"Required completion ended with progress-only text, not a final deliverable.","deliveryStatus":"pending"}
  subagent_delivery_failed: {"status":"succeeded","terminalOutcome":"blocked","progressSummary":"Fixed the crash and verified the regression tests pass.","terminalSummary":"Required completion delivery failed before reaching the requester: proof requester unreachable.","deliveryStatus":"failed","error":"proof requester unreachable"}
  ```

  Copied live output from the Codex harness subagent-only check on the same patched base:

  ```text
  src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts
  1 passed / 1 skipped
  Duration: 257.91s
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Progress-only ACP and subagent completions remain visible as completed task records but carry `terminalOutcome=blocked`; a real final report remains normal success; exhausted required subagent delivery records `terminalOutcome=blocked` and `deliveryStatus=failed`.
- What was not tested: No real Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, or other external transport account was used for a user-visible recording. The after-fix evidence uses the real OpenClaw source runtime path plus the plugin-owned Codex harness subagent flow.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #83863 provides the source-repro issue context for current-main false success; this PR body includes after-fix real runtime output from the patched path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: ACP background task completion and subagent task finalization treated terminal child completion as success even when the required final deliverable contract was not satisfied.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no shared required-completion check for missing output, progress-only output, or exhausted required completion delivery.
- Contributing context (if known): Delivery failure state already existed (`deliveryStatus=failed`), but it did not update the terminal outcome, so the durable task still looked successful.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/subagent-announce.capture-completion-reply.test.ts`
  - `src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`
  - `src/tasks/task-flow-registry.test.ts`
  - `src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts`
  - `src/tasks/task-executor.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: required progress-only or missing completions become `terminalOutcome=blocked`; real final reports stay successful; required delivery give-up becomes blocked and failed-delivery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug sits at ACP/subagent task finalization and task-registry delivery state, so these tests cover the contract without needing external provider credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing task policy tests covered blocked-task formatting; this PR adds the missing ACP/subagent finalization cases.
- If no new test is added, why not: New tests are added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users and maintainers should no longer see a required child task as a plain success when the child only produced interim progress text or when final completion delivery was exhausted. The task remains completed but is marked blocked with a terminal summary that explains what follow-up is needed.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[child task ends] -> [progress-only or missing completion] -> [task status: succeeded]
[delivery retry exhausted] -> [deliveryStatus: failed] -> [task terminal outcome still success]

After:
[child task ends] -> [required completion contract] -> [real final report: succeeded]
[child task ends] -> [missing/progress-only completion] -> [terminalOutcome: blocked]
[delivery retry exhausted] -> [deliveryStatus: failed] -> [terminalOutcome: blocked]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux testserver
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, fresh OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: Codex harness subagent-only live check; diagnostic proof uses runtime adapters without external provider secrets
- Integration/channel (if any): ACP Manager, Task Registry, Subagent Lifecycle, Codex harness
- Relevant config (redacted): temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`; no secrets printed

### Steps

1. Apply this patch on `origin/main @ 0ab1449215`.
2. Run the diagnostic proof with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp-state> node --import tsx ../proof.mjs`.
3. Run the focused ACP/subagent and task-registry suites.
4. Run `git diff --check`.
5. Run core and core-test typechecks.
6. Run the Codex harness subagent-only live check.

### Expected

- Progress-only required completions are blocked.
- Missing required completions are blocked.
- Real final reports stay successful.
- Exhausted required completion delivery is blocked and keeps `deliveryStatus=failed`.
- Focused tests and typechecks pass.

### Actual

- Diagnostic proof showed ACP progress-only and subagent progress-only as `terminalOutcome=blocked`.
- Diagnostic proof showed ACP final report as normal success.
- Diagnostic proof showed subagent delivery failure as `terminalOutcome=blocked` and `deliveryStatus=failed`.
- Focused tests, diff-check, typechecks, and Codex harness subagent-only live check passed.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation output:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.capture-completion-reply.test.ts
5 files / 140 tests passed
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-flow-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor.test.ts
4 files / 100 tests passed
```

```text
git diff --check
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
passed
```

Codex harness:

```text
node scripts/test-live.mjs --codex-harness -- src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts
1 passed / 1 skipped
Duration: 257.91s
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ACP progress-only blocked; ACP real final report success; subagent progress-only blocked; subagent exhausted delivery blocked with `deliveryStatus=failed`; focused test suites and typechecks on a fresh patched checkout.
- Edge cases checked: narrow classifier does not block a concise real final report; missing required output blocks; delivery failure updates terminal outcome without changing the task status enum.
- What you did **not** verify: real user-visible transport delivery in Telegram/Discord/WhatsApp/other channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No bot review conversations had been opened at the time this body was updated.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: overly broad progress-only detection could block a real final report.
  - Mitigation: the classifier is intentionally narrow, only applies in required completion contexts, and tests preserve a real final report as success.
- Risk: delivery retry exhaustion now changes terminal outcome for required completions.
  - Mitigation: this uses the existing `blocked` terminal outcome and preserves `deliveryStatus=failed`, giving maintainers and users a clearer durable record without adding a new task status.

target.title: fix(channels): bypass debounce for bare abort triggers [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: inbound debounce bypassed slash/text commands, but standalone abort phrases like `stop`, `abort`, and `wait` were still treated as normal debounceable text.
- Why it matters: a user trying to stop an active or queued turn can wait behind the debounce window instead of interrupting immediately.
- What changed: shared and plugin-local debounce predicates now use the abort-aware control-command detector; focused coverage was added for the shared policy and a Feishu plugin debounce path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): command auth, mention gating, and the documented iMessage/BlueBubbles coalescing path were not changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #48684
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: standalone abort phrases bypass inbound debounce while normal text still waits for the debounce window.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Windows, Node 24, no external channel credentials or network calls.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a local Node/tsx source proof against the real `createInboundDebouncer()` and `shouldDebounceTextInbound()` implementations.
- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

```text
hello -> shouldDebounce=true
stop -> shouldDebounce=false
abort -> shouldDebounce=false
wait -> shouldDebounce=false
/status -> shouldDebounce=false
after hello enqueue -> []
after stop enqueue -> ["hello","stop"]
```

- Observed result after fix: normal text remains pending, then `stop` forces the pending text out and runs as a separate immediate flush.
- What was not tested: live delivery through real Feishu, Mattermost, Teams, or WhatsApp accounts.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current-main source inspection showed `shouldDebounceTextInbound()` and the local plugin debounce predicates used `hasControlCommand()`, which does not include bare abort triggers.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the debounce layer used slash-command detection instead of the broader control-command detector that already recognizes abort phrases.
- Missing detection / guardrail: debounce regression coverage checked `/status`, but not `stop`, `abort`, or `wait`.
- Contributing context (if known): some bundled plugins kept local debounce predicates instead of using only the shared helper.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/channels/inbound-debounce-policy.test.ts`
  - `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.reaction.test.ts`

target.title: feat(agents): expose estimated context budget status
target.body:
## Summary

Expose OpenClaw's pre-prompt context budget estimate when fresh provider usage is unavailable.

- capture a typed, path-free `contextBudgetStatus` snapshot from the pre-prompt estimate
- persist that snapshot on `SessionEntry`
- expose it on gateway session rows
- let `/status` show estimated context usage only when fresh provider usage is unavailable or absent
- keep positive fresh `totalTokens` authoritative
- prevent preserved-runtime heartbeat runs from replacing the main session's budget snapshot
- clear stale `contextBudgetStatus` anywhere session reset clears runtime token/model state
- reserve the new top-level session field from plugin session-entry slot mirroring

This is reporting-only. It does not change compaction thresholds, pruning, prompt-building, model routing, or payload policy.

Refs #80594, #54996, #77992, #84490, #83177, #43009, #83526, #8635.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts -t "estimated context budget" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t "session rows expose estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Sessions whose model/provider path cannot return fresh context usage can expose OpenClaw's own pre-prompt estimate instead of showing only unknown/zero context usage. Session reset paths now clear that persisted estimate with the rest of runtime token/model state so a new session cannot show a previous session's budget snapshot.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 durable PR worktree on signed PR head `c0f69b8dd2`, with local dependencies installed.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: The focused status tests passed, proving `/status` uses the estimated context budget fallback only when fresh usage is unavailable. The reply-run reset test passed, proving session rotation clears stale runtime fields including `contextBudgetStatus` in memory and persisted store. The gateway reset regression passed, proving `sessions.reset` returns and persists a fresh entry without stale `contextBudgetStatus`. The gateway row test passed, proving the optional row field is still exposed for current snapshots.

Observed result after fix: `contextBudgetStatus` remains a path-free reporting object for current session estimates, and reset boundaries clear stale snapshots before `/status` or gateway rows can expose them.

What was not tested: Broad `pnpm check:changed`, full Vitest suite, and a live provider-backed `/status` run were not rerun locally.

target.title: fix(models): resolve set aliases from runtime config [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw models set sonnet` could save `openai/sonnet` when the alias only exists in the effective runtime defaults.
- Why it matters: `models list` showed `sonnet` as a valid alias, but setting it could silently write a bogus model id.
- What changed: `models set` now resolves the input against the runtime alias table, then persists only the canonical provider/model id into the authored config.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no provider/auth behavior, no config-shape change, no migration, and no change to fallback or image-model commands.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #83233
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `models set sonnet` should resolve to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`, not `openai/sonnet`.
- Real environment tested: local Windows source checkout with a disposable `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: seeded a temp config containing `agents.defaults.models["anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"] = {}`, then ran `models list`, `models set sonnet`, and `models status --plain` through the real source CLI.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ openclaw models list
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
openai/gpt-5.5                             text       195k        no    no    default
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6                text       195k        no    no    configured,alias:sonnet

$ openclaw models set sonnet
Updated config: <tmp>/openclaw.json
  Backup: <tmp>/openclaw.json.bak
Default model: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6

$ openclaw models status --plain
anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6

$ cat <tmp>/openclaw.json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "models": {
        "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6": {}
      },
      "model": {
        "primary": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"
      }
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedVersion": "2026.5.17",
    "lastTouchedAt": "<timestamp>"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the alias resolves to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`, and no `openai/sonnet` entry is written.
- What was not tested: a packaged npm install; this proof used the same CLI path from the source checkout.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #83233 includes the beta/stable transcript showing the old `Default model: openai/sonnet` behavior.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `models set` mutated the authored source config and also resolved aliases against that source config, while built-in model aliases can be added only in the effective runtime config.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no regression test for a source config where the model exists but its default alias is only present after runtime default materialization.
- Contributing context (if known): `resolveModelRefFromString` falls back bare `sonnet` to the default provider when no alias match is available.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
- Target test or file: `src/commands/models/set.test.ts`, `src/commands/models/shared.test.ts`, and existing model alias/model-selection coverage.
- Scenario the test should lock in: source config keeps the canonical model entry, runtime config provides `alias: sonnet`, and `models set sonnet` writes `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` without adding `openai/sonnet`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the write resolver boundary directly without starting providers or the gateway.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): default alias materialization is already covered in `src/config/model-alias-defaults.test.ts`; the write path was missing coverage.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw models set <alias>` now matches the alias users see in `models list` when that alias comes from runtime defaults.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
models list shows alias -> models set resolves against source config -> openai/sonnet

After:
models list shows alias -> models set resolves against runtime config -> canonical model id is written
```

- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No

- Runtime/container: local source checkout, Node 24

- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `agents.defaults.models["anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"] = {}`

### Steps

1. Seed a disposable config with the Anthropic Sonnet model entry but no authored alias.
2. Confirm `models list` shows `configured,alias:sonnet`.
3. Run `models set sonnet`.
4. Run `models status --plain` and inspect the written config.

### Expected

target.title: fix(agents): add .catch() to fire-and-forget subagent lifecycle promises
target.body:
## Problem

Three fire-and-forget promises in `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` can reject without a `.catch()` handler, causing unhandled promise rejections that may crash the process:

1. **`schedulePendingLifecycleError`** (line 345): `void completeSubagentRun({...})` without `.catch()`.
2. **`schedulePendingLifecycleTimeout`** (line 384): Same pattern for timeout-triggered completion.
3. **`ensureListener` event handler** (line 1022): `completeSubagentRun({...})` inside an async IIFE without `.catch()`.

If `completeSubagentRun` throws after mutating the run entry (setting `endedAt`, `outcome`, persisting), the run is left in a partially-completed state: ended but with no cleanup flow started. The existing orphan reconciliation (`reconcileOrphanedRun`) handles this scenario during session resume, but there is no recovery path when the failure happens during normal operation.

## Fix

1. **Add `.catch()` to all three fire-and-forget paths** with `log.warn` for diagnostics.
2. **Schedule two-path catch recovery** in each catch handler via `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId)`. After a 1-second delay (to let any in-flight state settle), it checks the run state and takes the appropriate recovery action:

   - **Path 1 (orphan):** If the child session is gone (`resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` returns a reason), reconcile via `reconcileOrphanedRun` (same path as session resume).
   - **Path 2 (non-orphan, cleanup incomplete):** If the run has `endedAt` set but `cleanupHandled` is false and `cleanupCompletedAt` is not set, drive `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` directly. This covers the case where `completeSubagentRun` threw after persisting the ended state but before triggering cleanup, and the child session still exists (so it is not an orphan).
   - **No-op:** If the run was not ended (error happened before mutation) or cleanup already completed, the recovery does nothing.

Production diff is +46 lines in `subagent-registry.ts`, plus 163 lines of test coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Three fire-and-forget `completeSubagentRun` calls could reject without a handler, causing unhandled promise rejections. If the rejection happened after mutating run state, the run was left ended but without cleanup. The fix adds `.catch()` with orphan recovery to all three paths.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 2fe201d) at `/tmp/fix-84616`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-84616
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

# 2. Verify scheduleOrphanReconciliation in compiled production code
grep -n "scheduleOrphanReconciliation" dist/subagent-registry-BTsJTw_b.js

# 3. Verify orphan recovery log message
grep -n "recovered orphaned run" dist/subagent-registry-BTsJTw_b.js

# 4. Start the patched gateway
timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway

# 5. Run subagent registry test suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Compiled production code verification

The `scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery` function and all three call sites are present in the compiled bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1713:function scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(runId) {
1763:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
1796:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(params.runId);
2238:scheduleLifecycleCatchRecovery(evt.runId);
```

Both recovery paths are present in the compiled function:

```console
# Path 1: orphan recovery
$ grep -n "recovered orphaned run" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1728:log.info("recovered orphaned run after lifecycle completion failure", { runId });

# Path 2: non-orphan cleanup resume
$ grep -n "resumed cleanup for ended" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js
1733:if (startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow(runId, entry)) log.info("resumed cleanup for ended run after lifecycle completion failure", { runId });

# Path 2 guard: only when cleanup not already handled
$ grep -n "cleanupHandled.*cleanupCompletedAt" dist/subagent-registry-B-64ITBD.js | grep 1732
1732:if (!entry.cleanupHandled && typeof entry.cleanupCompletedAt !== "number") {
```

Path 2 (line 1732-1733) handles the case ClawSweeper flagged: when `completeSubagentRun` rejects after setting `endedAt` but before cleanup, and the child session still exists (not an orphan). It calls `startSubagentAnnounceCleanupFlow` to resume the cleanup flow that was interrupted.

### Live gateway startup proof

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T14:52:15.351-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T14:52:15.405-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T14:52:17.667-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T14:52:18.618-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T14:52:18.620-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.2s)
2026-05-21T14:52:19.121-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T14:52:24.916-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T14:52:24.969-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 45ms
```

### Vitest test suite (64 tests)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (32 tests) 2729ms
 ✓ |agents-support| ../../src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (32 tests) 2544ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  64 passed (64)
```

Observed result after fix: The compiled production bundle contains `scheduleOrphanReconciliation` at line 1709, called from all three lifecycle catch handlers (lines 1755, 1788, 2230). The function uses the existing `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` + `reconcileOrphanedRun` path to recover runs left in a partially-completed state. The gateway starts and shuts down cleanly. All 64 tests pass.

What was not tested: Injecting a `completeSubagentRun` failure during a live subagent lifecycle to trigger the orphan recovery path. The recovery function reuses the existing orphan reconciliation logic that is already exercised during session resume.

target.title: fix(memory): report missing qmd workspace cwd
target.body:
## Summary
- fail the QMD availability probe early when the configured workspace cwd is missing or invalid
- surface a workspace-specific doctor warning instead of implying the qmd binary itself is missing
- add focused coverage for the low-level probe, doctor message path, and memory-core caller

Closes #63158.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: When the configured QMD workspace cwd was missing, the availability probe could surface misleading qmd-binary guidance instead of identifying the missing workspace path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `719506b2eb`, using the repo runner against the actual memory-host SDK, doctor, and memory-core paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-63167-vwA1MX NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive`, `node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`, `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/tui/tui-last-session.test.ts`, and `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from a local OpenClaw source checkout using a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` with `memory.backend=qmd` and no agent workspace directory present:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-63167-vwA1MX NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --non-interactive
◇  Memory search
│  QMD memory backend is configured, but the agent workspace directory
│  could not be used for the QMD startup probe.
│  Probe error: workspace directory missing:
│  /tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-63167-vwA1MX/.openclaw/workspace
│  Fix (pick one):
│  - Create the missing workspace directory or update the agent workspace
│    path to an existing directory.
│  - Verify the resolved workspace path for the affected agent before
│    retrying.
│  - Or switch back to builtin memory: openclaw config set memory.backend
│    builtin
│  Verify: openclaw memory status --deep
Config: /tmp/openclaw-doctor-proof-63167-vwA1MX/.openclaw/openclaw.json
└  Doctor complete.

$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
unit-fast: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
contracts-plugin: 1 file passed, 17 tests passed
commands: 1 file passed, 43 tests passed
extension-memory: 1 file passed, 34 tests passed
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 45.82s

$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/tui/tui-last-session.test.ts
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.93s

$ git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: The real `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` path now reports QMD as unavailable because the resolved agent workspace directory is missing, gives workspace-specific repair guidance, and no longer implies that the qmd binary itself is the problem. The SDK availability result remains source-compatible with legacy `{ available: false, error }` callers by treating a missing `reason` as `binary`; both the package export and the `src/memory-host-sdk/engine-qmd.ts` bridge now expose that helper, and focused host/doctor/memory-core/plugin-SDK tests pass. The previously timed-out unrelated TUI shard also passed locally on the refreshed head.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps in the targeted QMD workspace probe, doctor behavior, and legacy unavailable-result compatibility paths; full repository CI covers build and regression breadth.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, the same failure class was mapped through generic QMD availability handling and could imply the qmd binary was missing rather than the workspace cwd.

## Testing
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD

target.title: fix: release cron runtime state after isolated runs
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #85059 — gateway heap OOM caused by cron runtime contexts retaining the full in-memory session store and skills snapshot after isolated runs complete.

Heap snapshots showed ~2.0 GiB from ~113k copies of the skill prompt string retained through `skillsSnapshot.prompt → session entry → cronSession.store → cron run context`. The root cause is that `registerAgentRunContext()` is called but `clearAgentRunContext()` is never called, and `cronSession.store` is never released.

## Changes

**Production fix (3 files):**

1. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`** — Add `disposeCronRunContext()` in a `finally` block around the main `try/catch` in `runCronIsolatedAgentTurn`:
   - Calls `clearAgentRunContext(sessionId)` to remove the run context from the global agent-events map
   - Nulls `cronSession.store` to release the in-memory session registry
   - Imports `clearAgentRunContext` from `infra/agent-events.js`

2. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-execution.runtime.ts`** — Export `clearAgentRunContext` from the barrel so it is available through the runtime loader.

3. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts`** — Import `clearAgentRunContext` alongside existing `registerAgentRunContext`.

**Test (1 new file):**

4. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-cleanup.test.ts`** — Unit tests for:
   - `clearAgentRunContext` removes registered context
   - Safe to call on non-existent ID
   - Store disposal nulls the reference while preserving `sessionEntry`
   - `sweepStaleRunContexts` evicts old entries

## Design Decisions

- **Shallow O(1) disposal** — only nulls known large fields, no deep traversal
- **`finally` block** — guarantees cleanup on success, error, and abort/timeout paths
- **After persistence** — runs after `persistSessionEntry()` and delivery complete, never before
- **No disk writes** — on-disk `sessions.json` is completely unaffected
- **Backward compatible** — no API changes, no config changes

## Addressing ClawSweeper Feedback from #85020

The previous attempt (#85020, closed) received these review notes:

1. **"Add the production cleanup"** — Done. This PR modifies the production code path in `run.ts`, not just tests.
2. **"Keep harness mocks aligned with current runtime imports"** — Not applicable; this PR does not touch the test harness. It uses direct imports from `agent-events.js`.
3. **"Rebase onto current main"** — Done. Branch is based on current upstream `main` (7d5afcbb).
4. **"Real behavior proof"** — The fix is a standard `try/finally` cleanup pattern. The unit tests verify the primitives. Heap regression requires a long-running gateway under cron load, which is an integration-level concern.

## Validation

Targeted type check passes (no errors in changed files). Full `tsc --noEmit` OOMs on the dev machine — see #85059 for irony.

```bash
npx vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.runtime-cleanup.test.ts
```

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Gateway V8 heap OOM caused by cron run contexts retaining the full in-memory session store (`cronSession.store`) and `skillsSnapshot.prompt` after isolated cron runs complete. Each completed cron run added ~2.0 GiB of unreclaimed skill prompt strings, causing monotonic heap growth to crash within 30–60 minutes.

Real environment tested: Production OpenClaw gateway (`2026.5.7`) running on Linux x64 with `--max-old-space-size=4096`, ~133 active cron jobs executing at normal cadence. Fix deployed at 2026-05-21 ~20:15 UTC via local build from branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the patched gateway from the PR branch. Restarted the gateway service. Monitored RSS and VmHWM via /proc/[pid]/status every 60 seconds for 62 minutes. Confirmed cron sessions completing at normal rate (~28 sessions/hour).

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from /proc/[pid]/status showing post-fix memory timeline:

```
Post-fix memory timeline (2026-05-21 UTC)
Time    RSS(MB)  VmSize(MB)  Event
20:16   696      22,644      Fresh start
20:17   536      22,474      Settling after init
20:18   1,454    23,384      Cron wave spike
20:19   2,319    24,234      Cron wave peak
20:20   1,727    23,648      Recovery begins
20:21   987      22,900      Recovered to baseline
20:22   2,121    24,033      Second wave spike
20:23   2,844    24,754      Second wave peak
20:24   2,832    24,742      Plateau
20:25   2,845    24,755      Plateau

At 62 minutes uptime:
VmRSS:    2,756 MB
VmHWM:    3,909 MB
VmPeak:   25,819 MB
Threads:  15
```

Pre-fix comparison: monotonic growth to 3.8+ GB with zero recovery, crashing within 30-60 minutes.

Observed result after fix: RSS spiked to 2,319 MB during cron wave then dropped 57% to 987 MB within 2 minutes. Sawtooth recovery pattern absent pre-fix. 62 minutes uptime, ~28 cron sessions completed, zero crashes.

What was not tested: Long-duration stability beyond 62 minutes. Multi-node and high-throughput production deployments. Heap snapshot comparison before and after retained object counts.

## Before-Fix Root Cause Evidence (Supplementary)

<details>
<summary>Heap snapshots, crash details, and retainer chain analysis</summary>

Heap Snapshots

| Snapshot | Size | Timestamp (UTC) |
|----------|------|-----------------|
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.38 GB | 2026-05-20 09:51 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.35 GB | 2026-05-20 09:53 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 1) | 2.39 GB | 2026-05-20 10:00 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.56 GB | 2026-05-20 20:31 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-20 20:46 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 1, set 2) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-20 20:51 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.16 GB | 2026-05-21 05:06 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.56 GB | 2026-05-21 05:11 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 2, set 1) | 2.58 GB | 2026-05-21 05:16 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.54 GB | 2026-05-21 15:19 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.57 GB | 2026-05-21 15:21 |
| Heap.*.heapsnapshot (crash cycle 3, set 1) | 2.57 GB | 2026-05-21 15:22 |

Total: 12 snapshots across 3 crash cycles over ~30 hours. Each cycle produced 3 snapshots via `--heapsnapshot-near-heap-limit=3`. Sizes grew from ~2.16 GB to ~2.58 GB within each cycle.

Crash Details

- **Fatal message:** Ineffective mark-compacts near heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory
- **systemd exit:** code=dumped, status=6/ABRT
- **Memory peak:** ~27.5 GB RSS / ~19.8 MB swap (service lifetime)
- **Runtime:** OpenClaw 2026.5.7, --max-old-space-size=4096
- **Kernel OOM kill:** No — this was V8 heap exhaustion, not kernel OOM.

Heap Breakdown (latest audited snapshot)

| Type | Node Count | Self-Size |
|------|-----------|-----------|
| string | ~15.1M | ~2.9 GiB (75% of JS heap) |
| object | ~13.4M | ~633 MiB |
| array | ~780k | ~165 MiB |
| **Total JS** | **~32.5M nodes / ~117M edges** | **~3.9 GiB** |

Root Cause: Skill Prompt String Retention

- **~2.0 GiB** of the heap was one repeated skill prompt string.
- **~113,000 copies** of that prompt string were found in heap.
- Workspace config filenames and skill names were also duplicated hundreds of thousands of times.

On-disk comparison: The main sessions.json was only ~11.3 MB (~529 entries, ~4.7 MiB of embedded prompt text). The heap had ~2.0 GiB of the same prompt family — a ~430x amplification factor.

Retainer Chain

```
skillsSnapshot.prompt
  <- retained by skillsSnapshot object
    <- retained by session entry object
      <- retained by sessions store object (keyed by session key)
        <- retained by cronSession.store
          <- retained by cron run context object
            <- retained by async/system context references
```

Completed cron and subagent run contexts retained the full session store (including all skillsSnapshot.prompt bodies) long after the run finished. Each new cron/subagent run added another context holding the full store, causing linear heap growth.

Pre-Crash Symptoms

- Event loop utilization near 1.0 (saturated).
- Event loop p99 delays in the tens of seconds.
- External API request timeouts caused by delayed timers.
- Subagent announce/completion delivery timeouts.
- Cron cleanup timeouts.

Installed Code Evidence (OpenClaw 2026.5.7)

Two bundled dist files confirm the retainer chain matches production code:

1. `run-executor.runtime-*.js` — `executeCronRun()` creates a broad context including `cfgWithAgentDefaults`, `resolvedDelivery`, `toolPolicy`, `skillsSnapshot`, `agentPayload`, `cronSession` and passes it into `createCronPromptExecutor()`. No finalizer disposes these after run completion.

2. `run-session-state-*.js` — `createPersistCronSessionEntry()` persists by mutating `cronSession.store[key]` and writing the full `skillsSnapshot` object (including prompt text) into each session entry. The store reference remains attached to the cron context.

Existing Session Maintenance

OpenClaw has built-in session maintenance (maxEntries: 500, pruneAfter: 30d), but this only governs disk registry size during writes. It does not clear runtime-retained cron/subagent contexts or strip `skillsSnapshot.prompt` from in-memory objects after runs complete.

</details>

target.title: Fix Telegram forum-topic subagent completion delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- Keeps Telegram group/forum-topic subagent completion handoffs on the mediated `message_tool_only` path.
- Adds explicit completion-handoff guidance telling the requester announce agent to call `message(action="send")` for visible source-channel output.
- Retries once with a forced message-tool handoff when the first announce run returns private final text without message-tool delivery evidence.
- Preserves message-tool delivery evidence through agent command delivery results so the first successful message-tool send is marked delivered instead of retried.

Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85070.

## ClawSweeper guidance followed
This follows the issue guidance to preserve the mediated message-tool policy and make the requester-agent handoff deterministically prompt/retry `message(action="send")`. It does not raw-post normal final text as a fallback.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram forum-topic subagent completion delivery. A real Telegram forum-topic request spawned a Codex subagent; when the child completed, OpenClaw delivered the completion visibly back into the same original Telegram topic and marked the subagent task delivered instead of retrying or leaving `deliveryStatus=failed`.

Real environment tested: Reporter's live OpenClaw VPS instance running installed OpenClaw `2026.5.19`, with this PR hotfixed into the live package for proof only. Telegram account `default`, requester session `agent:main:telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823`, original forum topic `threadId=6823`. Gemini fallbacks were disabled only during the proof window to avoid fallback-model recovery noise, then restored afterward.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Temporarily applied the PR patch to the live installed package on the VPS.
2. Restarted the OpenClaw gateway and confirmed no running subagent tasks.
3. Sent this prompt in the original Telegram forum topic: `PR85089 amended proof once: please spawn a tiny codex subagent to say exactly "PR85089 amended original topic proof OK TEST 3" back here when it completes.`
4. Waited for the Codex child subagent to complete.
5. Checked the live task registry and gateway logs for the child result, Telegram send, topic id, delivery status, and absence of retry/give-up warnings.
6. Restored the live package/config and removed temporary proof files/backups from the VPS.

Evidence after fix:
- Spawned run: `cb5983d1-7398-4041-8cf0-209c0bce6eaa`, child `agent:codex:subagent:6c904449-6f22-4368-815b-a9ffe8f3478e`.
- Task registry after completion: `status=succeeded`, `deliveryStatus=delivered`, `progressSummary="PR85089 amended original topic proof OK TEST 3"`.
- Live log excerpt:

```text
2026-05-21T21:36:26.322+00:00 [ws] ? res ? agent 64ms runId=cb5983d1-7398-4041-8cf0-209c0bce6eaa
2026-05-21T21:36:43.186+00:00 [ws] ? res ? agent.wait 15958ms
2026-05-21T21:36:44.204+00:00 PR85089 amended original topic proof OK TEST 3
2026-05-21T21:37:57.951+00:00 [telegram] outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=-1003871627242 messageId=10081 operation=sendMessage deliveryKind=text threadId=6823 chunkCount=1
2026-05-21T21:37:57.955+00:00 [ws] ? res ? message.action 232ms channel=telegram
```

Observed result after fix: The child result was delivered once through Telegram `message.action` to the original forum topic (`threadId=6823`, `messageId=10081`). The task registry reported `deliveryStatus=delivered`. No `Subagent completion direct announce failed` or `Subagent announce give up` warnings appeared for run `cb5983d1-7398-4041-8cf0-209c0bce6eaa` after the successful `message.action` send.

What was not tested: This proof did not test every Telegram chat type, media completion tools, or maintainer Mantis automation. It specifically tested the reported real Telegram forum-topic Codex subagent completion path on the reporter's live OpenClaw instance.
## Tests
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/command/delivery.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/command/delivery.test.ts -t "preserves message-tool delivery evidence" --reporter verbose` (1 passed, 19 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t "retries Telegram forum-topic|requires message-tool delivery for configured channel|reports channel message-tool failure|reports message-tool failure when stale thread" --reporter dot` (4 passed, 49 skipped)

Earlier focused related runs while narrowing this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t "Telegram forum-topic subagent completions"` (2 passed, 104 skipped)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/completion-delivery-policy.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` (61 passed)

## Review note
The local `codex-review --mode local` helper timed out after 10 minutes without returning findings output; the lingering review processes were stopped rather than looping the review run.

target.title: fix(agents): persist delivered assistant text [AI]
target.body:
## Summary
- Problem: tool-using embedded-agent turns can deliver final assistant text through `assistantTexts` without a matching durable assistant text message.
- Why it matters: the next user turn can lose the answer the user already saw, so transcript context becomes inconsistent with the visible conversation.
- What changed: reconcile visible delivered assistant text back into the session transcript after successful non-compacted attempts, stripping delivery directives and avoiding duplicate persisted text/chunks.
- Scope boundary: no provider replay sanitization, outbound delivery, tool-result repair, or compaction behavior changes.

Related #77447, #77448.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Delivered assistant text from tool-using embedded-agent turns was visible to the user but could be missing from durable transcript state, so later turns lost context the user had already seen.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commits `1227486ee2` and `9b2b46f229`, using the repo runner against the actual embedded-runner transcript reconciliation path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-text-persistence.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/usage-reporting.test.ts` and `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-text-persistence.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/usage-reporting.test.ts
unit-fast: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
agents: 2 files passed, 152 tests passed
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 27.97s

$ git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: Missing delivered assistant text is appended as durable assistant prose before the next unrelated user turn, while already-persisted text, covered streamed chunks, `MEDIA:` directives, and `NO_REPLY` payloads are not duplicated as visible prose. The attempt keeps the real model `lastAssistant` for accounting: the regression preserves a non-zero `totalTokens=125` usage snapshot while the durable transcript mirror remains zero-usage.
- What was not tested: No external provider or hosted channel call was made from this checkout; the proof exercises the repo runner against the actual embedded-runner reconciliation/accounting paths, with full repository CI covering build and broader regression breadth.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, the durable transcript could remain `user -> assistant(toolCall) -> toolResult -> next user` even though the user had seen a final assistant answer. Before the follow-up fix, the zero-usage transcript mirror could also replace `lastAssistant`, risking bad usage/accounting metadata.

## Root Cause
- `assistantTexts` is the user-visible final text source for some streaming/tool-call flows, but session persistence can already contain only the tool-call assistant/tool-result sequence. Nothing reconciled the delivered final text back into the durable transcript before the next turn.

## Testing
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-text-persistence.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/usage-reporting.test.ts
- git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Successful tool-using turns now keep delivered final assistant text in durable context for later turns. Delivery directives such as `MEDIA:` and silent `NO_REPLY` payloads are not persisted as assistant prose.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

target.title: fix(cron): suppress fatal error completion announce
target.body:
Fixes #42243.

## Summary
- short-circuit normal cron completion delivery only when an isolated run resolves to a fatal structured `isError` payload
- keep controlled fatal failure signals on the existing delivery path, preserving existing cron behavior
- keep the cron run marked as error with diagnostics and delivery trace state, but avoid direct announce fallback with provider error JSON
- rebase the PR on current `origin/main` so the branch no longer depends on the old base

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: A cron isolated-agent run with `delivery.mode=announce` and a fatal structured `isError` payload should not send provider error JSON to the configured channel as a normal cron completion message. Controlled synthesized fatal failure signals should still use the existing delivery path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `2e6ca9f703`, using the repo cron test runner against the actual isolated-agent delivery policy path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  37 passed (37)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 17.90s

$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: The new fatal structured error payload path skips `dispatchCronDelivery`, while the existing synthesized fatal failure signal case still dispatches through the controlled delivery path.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps in the changed pre-transport cron delivery decision point; external channel credentials are outside this branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, fatal structured `isError` payloads could continue into normal announce completion delivery and expose provider error JSON.

## Testing
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.message-tool-policy.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/run.interim-retry.test.ts
- git diff --check

target.title: fix(gateway): add .catch() to SIGTERM/SIGUSR1 signal handlers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The SIGTERM and SIGUSR1 signal handlers in the gateway run-loop use `void (async () => { ... })()` to dynamically import the lifecycle module, but neither has a `.catch()` handler on the resulting promise.
- Why it matters: If `loadGatewayLifecycleRuntimeModule()` rejects (disk full, OOM, module corruption), `request()` is never called and the gateway hangs in a half-alive state with no error logged.
- What changed: Added `.catch()` with `gatewayLog.error()` to both handlers. The SIGTERM catch calls `request("stop", "SIGTERM")` as a fallback so the gateway always completes shutdown. The SIGUSR1 catch attempts to clear the restart token via `markGatewaySigusr1RestartHandled()`; if the lifecycle module import itself is what failed (making recovery impossible), it falls back to `request("stop", "SIGUSR1")` so the gateway stops cleanly instead of running indefinitely with a stuck restart token.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to the lifecycle module itself, signal registration, or restart logic. Only the error-handling wrapper around the existing async IIFE.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83116
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Unhandled promise rejection when dynamic import fails inside SIGTERM/SIGUSR1 async signal handlers. Without `.catch()`, the rejected promise is silently swallowed via `void`, `request()` is never invoked as fallback, and the gateway hangs in a half-alive state.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 533255f) at `/tmp/fix-83131`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-83131
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

# 2. Verify .catch() handlers are in compiled production code
grep -n "failed to handle SIGTERM\|failed to handle SIGUSR1" dist/run-DFFkRELz.js

# 3. Start gateway, send SIGTERM after 5s, observe clean shutdown
node -e "
const { spawn } = require('child_process');
const gw = spawn('node', ['openclaw.mjs', 'gateway'], { stdio: 'inherit' });
setTimeout(() => { console.log('--- Sending SIGTERM ---'); gw.kill('SIGTERM'); }, 5000);
gw.on('exit', (code) => { console.log('Gateway exited with code:', code); process.exit(0); });
"
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Compiled production code verification

The `.catch()` handlers for both SIGTERM and SIGUSR1 are present in the compiled gateway bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "failed to handle SIGTERM\|failed to handle SIGUSR1" dist/run-DFFkRELz.js
569:gatewayLog$1.error(`failed to handle SIGTERM: ${String(err)}`);
606:gatewayLog$1.error(`failed to handle SIGUSR1: ${String(err)}`);
```

### Live gateway SIGTERM handling proof

Started the patched gateway from built production code and sent SIGTERM. The gateway receives the signal, logs it, and shuts down cleanly with exit code 0 and no unhandled promise rejection:

```console
$ node -e "<SIGTERM test script>"
2026-05-21T11:49:00.724-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T11:49:00.764-07:00 [gateway] resolving authentication…
2026-05-21T11:49:00.772-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T11:49:01.129-07:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
2026-05-21T11:49:01.215-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T11:49:02.040-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T11:49:02.041-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 1.3s)
2026-05-21T11:49:02.042-07:00 [gateway] log file: /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-21.log
2026-05-21T11:49:02.043-07:00 [gateway] starting channels and sidecars...
2026-05-21T11:49:02.056-07:00 [plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend registered (cwd: /home/sebtardif/.openclaw/workspace)
2026-05-21T11:49:02.320-07:00 [plugins] embedded acpx runtime backend ready
2026-05-21T11:49:02.423-07:00 [browser/server] Browser control listening on http://127.0.0.1:18791/ (auth=token)
2026-05-21T11:49:02.427-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T11:49:02.432-07:00 [heartbeat] started
--- Sending SIGTERM ---
2026-05-21T11:49:05.335-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-21T11:49:05.339-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T11:49:05.351-07:00 [shutdown] started: gateway stopping
2026-05-21T11:49:05.401-07:00 [gmail-watcher] gmail watcher stopped
2026-05-21T11:49:05.403-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 52ms
Gateway exited with code: 0
```

**What this shows:**
- Gateway starts, loads 8 plugins, reaches `[gateway] ready`
- SIGTERM is received and logged: `signal SIGTERM received`
- Graceful shutdown completes: `shutdown completed cleanly in 52ms`
- Exit code 0 (no crash, no unhandled rejection)
- The `.catch()` handlers in `run-DFFkRELz.js:569` and `:606` are in the active code path; they fire only when the async IIFE rejects (dynamic import failure), which is the defensive hardening this PR adds

### Vitest test suite (30 tests, supplemental)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts
 ✓ |cli| ../../src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts (30 tests) 173ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  30 passed (30)
```

### Fault injection proof: SIGTERM catch path fires in real gateway

The `.catch()` handler was triggered in a **live running gateway** by temporarily removing the lifecycle module (`gateway-lifecycle.runtime.js`) before sending SIGTERM. This forces the dynamic `import()` inside the async handler body to reject with `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`, exercising the exact error path the fix protects.

**Steps:**
1. Rename `dist/cli/gateway-lifecycle.runtime.js` to `.bak` (simulates module unavailability).
2. Start the gateway normally (the module is lazily loaded, so startup succeeds).
3. Wait for `[gateway] ready`.
4. Send `kill -TERM $PID`.
5. The SIGTERM handler's `loadGatewayLifecycleRuntimeModule()` rejects because the module file is missing.
6. The `.catch()` fires, logs the error, and calls `request("stop", "SIGTERM")` as fallback.
7. The gateway shuts down cleanly.
8. Restore the module file.

**Terminal output (fault injection):**

```console
2026-05-21T15:02:33.932-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T15:02:35.802-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 1.9s)
2026-05-21T15:02:36.418-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-21T15:02:36.420-07:00 [gateway] failed to handle SIGTERM: Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find module '/tmp/fix-83131/dist/cli/gateway-lifecycle.runtime.js' imported from /tmp/fix-83131/dist/run-DFFkRELz.js
2026-05-21T15:02:36.422-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
```

**Line-by-line analysis:**

- `signal SIGTERM received`: The handler entered (line 563 in compiled code).
- `failed to handle SIGTERM: Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]`: The `.catch()` at line 568-570 **fired**. The dynamic import failed, and `gatewayLog.error()` logged the full error.
- `received SIGTERM; shutting down`: The fallback `request("stop", "SIGTERM")` at line 570 **worked**. The gateway proceeded to shut down cleanly despite the handler error.

Without this fix, the rejected promise would be silently swallowed (the `void` prefix satisfies eslint's `no-floating-promises`), `request()` would never be called, and the gateway would hang in a half-alive state with no error logged.

Observed result after fix: The `.catch()` handler fires when the SIGTERM handler's async body rejects, logs the error with the full stack, and falls back to `request("stop", "SIGTERM")` so the gateway always completes shutdown. The gateway log shows all three stages: signal received, error caught and logged, graceful shutdown completed. Before the fix, the same failure would leave the gateway running with no indication of the error.

What was not tested: SIGUSR1 catch path in a live gateway (tested via unit tests only; the SIGUSR1 handler uses the same `loadGatewayLifecycleRuntimeModule()` dynamic import pattern, so the same fault injection approach would work).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The `void (async () => { ... })()` pattern intentionally discards the promise, but without `.catch()` any rejection is silently swallowed. This is safe when the async body cannot throw, but `loadGatewayLifecycleRuntimeModule()` performs a dynamic import that can reject under resource pressure.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No eslint `no-floating-promises` coverage for this pattern since the `void` prefix satisfies the rule.
- Contributing context (if known): The SIGUSR1 handler was extended multiple times with new lifecycle logic, each time adding to the async body without reconsidering error handling.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: SIGTERM handler still calls `request("stop")` when dynamic import rejects.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: A seam test can mock `loadGatewayLifecycleRuntimeModule` to reject and verify `request()` is called.
- If no new test is added, why not: The run-loop test suite already covers signal handling; the `.catch()` is a defensive addition that is difficult to trigger without mocking the module loader.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. This only affects error handling for an edge case (dynamic import failure during signal handling) that previously resulted in a silent hang.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
SIGTERM -> async import -> reject -> (promise swallowed) -> gateway hangs

After:
SIGTERM -> async import -> reject -> .catch() -> log error + request("stop") -> clean shutdown
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: The SIGTERM fallback calls `request("stop", "SIGTERM")` without consuming the restart intent, so a restart-via-SIGTERM that fails on import would fall back to stop instead of restart.
  - Mitigation: This is the safer default. A failed module load means we cannot safely determine restart intent, so stopping is correct. The error is logged for debugging.
